description: Customer-side skill that turns a brain package prepared by a Motion CSM into a populated org brain. Reads the package from ./uploads/ or /agent/brain/_sources/, runs live pulls against the customer's Motion workspace plus customer-wired integrations like Yotpo, runs Apify scrapers for organic web context, and synthesizes ten brain domains with source citations on every line. Triggered by phrases like 'build my brain' or 'run brain-onboard' after the CSM uploads the package.

installs.purpose: Core brain-onboard skill. Triggered by user prompts like 'build my brain' or 'run brain-onboard' after the CSM uploads the brain package.
Plain-language guide for the team on what to do when the CSM sends them a brain package.

changelog.notes: Add the operational layer: three registries (integrations/, routines/, apps/) that sit as siblings to the ten brain domains, the onboarding/ foundational layer, and signals/. Clean mental model — domains = brand knowledge, signals/ = forward-looking intelligence, integrations/ + routines/ + apps/ = what's connected, what runs, what's been built. integrations/ mirrors the internal integration-capabilities-library pattern (one folder per integration + README index) and is seeded at install from the manifest's wired_customer_integrations (connected) and tools_team_uses_not_yet_in_runneth (recommended); each entry records which brain domains it feeds, so data lineage is auditable from the brain side. It's kept current by the oauth_connection automation turn / integration-onboarding, which already writes the capabilities-and-scopes.md shape. routines/ is the routine registry Reza's example tree draws that the brain was missing (one file per routine + README), each entry naming its write target to close the loop on the routines-write-signals architecture; solves the same audit problem as the internal routine-storage-audit. apps/ is the app registry (README index) recording the durable state each app reads so apps stay views-over-state. New Standing rule 8 (operational layer is auditable, not decorative; rule 7 is v2.0.0's brain-content-vs-mechanics), brain-structure section expanded, new Step 4.Y (synthesis: populate integrations/ from the manifest, scaffold routines/ and apps/ READMEs even when empty, with _meta frontmatter and table shapes for all three), Step 6 welcome card adds one operational-layer readout line ('3 integrations wired, 4 recommended; 0 routines yet; 0 apps'), Step 6.5 onboarding-checklist integration and routine items now point at the registries, Step 7 _state.json adds operational_layer_populated plus registry counts, and the verify-install readout reports operational-layer status. Contained to brain-onboard; prep-customer-brain needs no change since it already ships the integration data in the manifest. Rebased onto v2.0.0 (operational layer is additive to the onboarding/ foundational layer).

Major restructure: add onboarding/ folder as a foundational brain layer alongside the ten domains and signals. The onboarding/ folder holds the anchor (why-motion.md), pain points, goals, success criteria, the checklist (moved from /agent/brain/onboarding-checklist.md root), team decisions, and per-call analysis logs. New Step 4.W reads the anchor and computes weights for downstream steps: which routines run, which integrations get prioritized, which signals populate, which capability cards lead. The anchor shapes SETUP, not creative output (briefs are grounded in domain content + signals). Step 6 welcome card now LEADS with the anchor (Why Motion + pain points in a dark ink callout) and surfaces onboarding-checklist status above the per-domain readout. Capability cards are weighted by which pain points each role addresses. Step 6.5 checklist gets two new sections (0: Anchor confirmation per Ale's ask, 1: Five-call onboarding sequence) and renumbers existing sections; lives at new path under onboarding/. Step 3.5 upgrade-safe path includes a v1.x→v2.0 migration step for the checklist relocation, idempotent and preserving team edits. Step 7 state schema records anchor_read_at and anchor_shapes_applied. Adds standing rule #7 (brain content vs brain mechanics) clarifying that the team's content ships customer-side while Motion's interpretations stay internal. Documents the calls/ split: structured findings ship in onboarding/calls/, full Gong analysis stays in customers/<slug>/_internal-context/calls/. Adds onboarding/calls-overview.md as a thin customer-facing explanation of the 5-call sequence. Step 6 "What's not in the brain yet" now shows WHY each recommended integration addresses a specific pain point from pain-points.md. Step 6.5 clarifies onboarding-progress-watcher runs customer-side. Apify moved to CSM-side: prep-customer-brain now runs the Apify scrapers (Reddit, IG, TikTok, Pinterest, YouTube, TrustPilot, Amazon, G2/Capterra, LinkedIn) during package prep with the same skip rules. Data ships in the package at _sources/apify/. Customer Runneths no longer need APIFY_API_KEY configured — one less integration to set up. Step 2B becomes a package read instead of a live actor pattern. Motion controls data quality, scraper selection, rate-limit handling, and Apify cost runs through Motion's account. Trade-off: Apify data refreshes only on prep runs (monthly typical), which is acceptable for Reddit/IG/TikTok organic and reviews.

Add Step 6.5 — Write the onboarding checklist. After the welcome card renders, brain-onboard writes /agent/brain/onboarding-checklist.md with the brand-agnostic continuation plan for the team and CSM to accomplish together. Five sections: brain enrichment (integrations + content confirmation + Motion gaps, items pulled from synthesis findings), operating habits (Slack channels, brain champion, refresh routines), first dopamine moments (each teammate asks a question, save-this used, first brief via signals, first failed answer routed through train channel), compound effect (weeks 2-4 wiring), forever loop (weekly/monthly/quarterly cadence). Sections 2-5 are template; section 1 items are pulled from real install findings. Team owns progress tracking. Welcome card 'What is not in the brain yet' section gets a one-line link to the full checklist. Also removes hardcoded Notion/Calendar and Drive/Notion/Library associations from Step 4 inputs table, Step 6 welcome card example copy, Step 6 readout rules, and Calendar/Library domain readout descriptions. brain-onboard never assumes a tool fills a specific domain. The team might use Google Calendar, Notion, Asana, or nothing for Calendar; Drive, Notion, Figma, or Frame.io for Library. Source pairings come from tools_team_uses_not_yet_in_runneth in the manifest and the refresh-config conversation, not from defaults. Originally v1.6.0; renumbered to v1.8.1 after v1.7.0 (signals) and v1.8.0 (SSH already-staged) merged first.

Reorder Step 1 to make "already staged at /agent/brain/_sources/" the primary path and reflect the new SSH delivery flow. The CSM uses runneth-cli locally to stream the package from their machine (sourced from Motion-Creative/customer_brain via gh) onto the customer VM. GitHub remains the canonical store and audit layer but is no longer the primary install-time source. GitHub clone path stays as a fallback for cases where SSH delivery didn't happen but CUSTOMER_BRAIN_READ_PAT is wired. Customer Runneths no longer need CUSTOMER_BRAIN_READ_PAT in the common case. Reference: linear SSH guide 83b71b2a92bf. Manual trigger of brain-onboard still required after SSH delivery; auto-trigger on file detection in /agent/brain/_sources/ is future work.

Canonicalize the signals/ layer surfaced during the Printfresh pilot. Eight standard files with brand-agnostic schemas and deterministic population rules: format-gaps (product × format whitespace), audience-gaps (untargeted segments × product fits), inspo-steals (competitor angles not in own rotation), seasonal-whitespace (upcoming occasions with no creative), review-requests (customer asks from VoC), organic-paid-gaps (organic themes absent from paid), hook-vocabulary (competitor language territories), graveyard-candidates (paused concepts to reconsider). Each file populates per install using the same logic against the brand's actual synthesized content. New Step 4.X documents the directory layout, common _meta frontmatter, per-file population rules, status semantics (populated/partial/empty-needs-upstream-content), refresh integration with routines, and briefing-chain integration. Empty signal files surface on the welcome card and in onboarding-checklist as "connect <source> to unlock <signal>". Welcome card Step 6 readout adds a Signals section sitting alongside the ten domains. Schemas live in the customer_brain repo at _signals-schemas/, not inlined in this skill. Custom per-customer schemas live at <customer-slug>/_signals-custom/. brain-onboard clones both at install and every refresh. This means new canonical signal types ship via PR to the customer_brain repo without needing a brain-onboard upgrade. Populated signals stay local at /agent/brain/signals/ in the customer Runneth (no push-back to repo since customer Runneths only have read access).

Replace welcome card structure: drop the "What it knows by heart" snapshot section and expand the per-domain readout to carry the same high-signal facts. New structure: (1) Header, (2) "What's in your brain by domain" with one section per domain leading with a headline number and 2-3 highlights, (3) Capability cards by role, (4) "What's not in the brain yet." The per-domain readout is the same information the old snapshot covered (KPI, top performer, headcount, brand positioning, customer voice, dominant hook) but anchored to where each fact lives in the brain. Each domain section ends with the brain path so the team knows where to look. Required per-domain content shape documented: identity (teammates + roles), brand (positioning + voice + products), customers (personas + themes + review counts), competition (confirmed brands + ad counts), performance (creatives + spend + KPI + top performer + best hook + best format + trend), strategy (KPI + windows + bets), calendar/library (pending OAuths), knowledge (glossary + decisions), preferences (empty on day one). Driven by Ankhway pilot.

Three changes: (1) Restore GitHub clone path in Step 1 as the PRIMARY package-staging path. Customer Runneth clones from Motion-Creative/customer_brain/<customer-slug>/ via CUSTOMER_BRAIN_READ_PAT. Tar and Drive paths stay as legacy fallbacks. (2) Remove motion meta age-gender from Step 2 pull list and remove age-gender.json from Performance domain inputs. (3) Add explicit never-run rule for Meta demographic analysis: demographics not load-bearing for creative strategy; personas come from VoC, not Meta-targeting. Supersedes the GitHub transport removal from PR #119; we are restoring it now that the CUSTOMER_BRAIN_READ_PAT secret is wired and the customer_brain repo flow is the canonical path (matches Ankhway example).

Six changes: (1) Add Step 1.5 — explicit install of bundled skills from _skills/. (2) Add fourth install-state case to Step 3.5: _state.json present but content lacks frontmatter triggers upgrade-safe path (surfaced by Printfresh install). (3) Make _changelog.md write requirement explicit in Step 4 with format example. (4) Add Brand domain products/ substructure: per-product spec.md, claims.md, catalogue README with sources from brand-audit, web crawl, HubSpot, Shopify. (5) Add Customers domain substructure: personas/<slug>.md per persona, reviews/by-source/by-product/by-theme/, ad-comments/by-creative/by-theme/ with top-20 sampling and volume management. (6) Add Performance domain aggregations: by-hook-tactic, by-hook-voice, by-visual-format, by-messaging-angle, by-persona, by-awareness-stage using motion ai-glossary tags joined to motion meta/tiktok insights.

Two changes: (1) Drop GitHub repo transport from Step 1 (supersedes PR #118). (2) Add Step 3.5 — upgrade detection and existing-content protection. When brain-onboard installs on a Runneth that already has brain content from an older version, the upgrade-safe path archives existing content, walks for user-saved candidates, surfaces findings on the welcome card for explicit team confirmation, and re-imports approved files with managed_by: user frontmatter before synthesis runs. Prevents silent loss of save-this-to-brain content during version upgrades.

Initial release. Customer-side skill that turns a CSM-prepared brain package into a populated ten-domain org brain. Reads the package from uploads or sources directory, runs Motion workspace pulls, runs Apify scrapers, pulls customer-wired integrations like Yotpo, and synthesizes the brain with source citations. Welcome card leads with what each person on the team can now do.
